_doc: Canonical step manifest for tools/readme-conformance-check.py. Each step has a stable step_id, a class, and a how_to_verify_done block. The gate reads this file; do not rename step_ids without migrating existing attestation/waiver files. Drift notes from the 2026-06-13 forensic are embedded as drift_note fields (informational only).
_credit_plane_advisory: P26 (metadata-only; no consumer key): audit-deep runs an ADVISORY serving-join lint (tools/credit-plane-lint.py over tools/lib/credit_evidence.py) that WARNs when genuine on-disk evidence (mutation-verified mvc sidecar / ws-owned hunt sidecar) was not credited by a downstream reader. Wave-1 is READ-ONLY: WARN + rc 0 by default; elevates to FAIL only under AUDITOOOR_CREDIT_PLANE_STRICT / --strict (never issued in audit-deep). NOT a runbook step, NOT wired into audit-done-guard. This key is pure descriptive metadata - no reader of this manifest iterates top-level keys (all readers access manifest['steps']).

_classes.mechanical: Fully automated - verified by presence/content of an artifact the tool writes.
_classes.conditional-mechanical: Automated path exists but requires an explicit operator flag or precondition; the tool checks the artifact AND a condition marker.
_classes.manual: Purely operator shell work - no auditooor make target does it. Requires an attestation file.
_classes.manual-judgment: Operator authors content requiring domain knowledge - no tool can validate quality. Requires an attestation file.
_classes.manual-judgment+conditional-mechanical-r: The authoring half is manual-judgment; the run half is mechanical once harnesses exist. Both must be attested.
_classes.step-2c-input: A load-bearing mechanical INPUT-materialization step for Step 2c: a tool derives a deterministic worklist artifact from prior-step artifacts without operator judgment.
_classes.orchestration: A load-bearing graph transition that validates or routes completed producer evidence but is not itself a reasoner.

steps.label: Clone src/ at audit pin and populate prior_audits/
steps.what_must_be_done: FIRST populate targets.tsv (one row per in-scope repo: repo_url<TAB>pinned_40_hex_commit<TAB>local_name) - this is BOTH the clone input for fetch-targets.sh AND the Tier-6 commit-mining input that step-1 `make audit` hard-requires (an empty/stub targets.tsv makes make audit fail-closed at the audit-target-commit-mining prerequisite). THEN clone each repo at its exact PIN via `bash tools/fetch-targets.sh` (or manually into src/<local_name>). Populate prior_audits/ with any published audit reports (drives R47/R53 dedup). ALSO ingest the program's OWN rules into two machine-readable artifacts (manual step; see docs/PROGRAM_INTAKE.md): (1) .auditooor/program_rules.json - PoC seeding requirements, program-excluded impact conditions (e.g. ONE_ASSET-dependence), eligibility.disclosed_unpatched_eligible (per-program: is a disclosed-but-live vuln eligible?), and the completed-audits list; (2) prior_audits/known_issues.jsonl - one row per DISCLOSED prior finding with fix_verified_at_pin (true|false|unknown - the 'is the exploit still LIVE at this pin' flag) + dedup_class. Verify with `python3 tools/program-intake-check.py --workspace <ws>`. These GROUND every dedup / scope / PoC-requirement decision (a candidate matching a disclosed dedup_class is a dupe unless R47/R53 extension-distinct; a program-excluded impact condition = invalid; fix_verified_at_pin=false on a disclosed-unpatched-eligible program = a fileable reverted-fix lead). ENFORCED downstream by pre-submit Check #145 (program-rules-check) + the early prior-audit dedup gate wired into spawn-worker.sh.

steps.what_must_be_done: LOAD-BEARING intake coverage-plane materialization. Run `make pipeline-intake-coverage-plane WS={workspace}`. steps.coverage_plane_note: IMPACT x MECHANISM / (unit x frame) SUBSTRATE (2026-07-01, all-languages): the pipeline has multiple parallel enumerate -> question -> hunt -> verdict generators (per-function hacker-Q, per-impact methodology, flow-seed, mechanism-scan) that historically kept DISJOINT ledgers, so a function cleared under one impact frame was treated as covered under all. The substrate unifies them via these ENFORCING/OPERATIONAL parts: (a) frame-keyed hunt sidecars (hunt__<unit>__I-<impact>) so a theft-frame verdict and a freeze-frame verdict on the same unit are distinct rows; (b) per-(function x impact) task generation - BOTH canonical hunt entrypoints (make hunt-scoped via per-fn-mimo-batch-gen.py, and make hunt-batch-bodypack via inscope-hunt-batch-builder.py) emit one task per applicable frame by DEFAULT, gated by the shared PER_IMPACT_FRAMES env knob (PER_IMPACT_FRAMES=0 reverts to one all-impacts pass); (c) per-(unit x frame) gate credit at audit-complete, enforced by tools/audit-completeness-check.py's check_completeness_matrix reading .auditooor/completeness_matrix.json (this is the load-bearing enforcement artifact - NOT the coverage_plane); (d) a mechanism-findings -> exploit-queue bridge so a fired mechanism detector becomes a hunt/exploit obligation rather than a dead disposition row. SEPARATELY, and NON-enforcing: (e) the vault_coverage_plane MCP callable plus its durable on-disk mirror .auditooor/coverage_plane.jsonl (producer: tools/coverage-plane-build.py, advisory-only in make audit-deep) are an ADVISORY inspection view DERIVED FROM completeness_matrix.json; no hunt builder and no gate (including audit-complete) reads coverage_plane. MONO-FOCUS LESSON: dispatch ONE frame per hunt task (a single kill_condition) - a multi-impact prompt dilutes the adversary goal and the off-frame bug (the strata freeze) slips past a theft-focused pass.

steps.scope_authority_note: SCOPE-AUTHORITY (strata 2026-07-01, generic/all-languages): the in-scope set authored here (SCOPE.md) and materialized into .auditooor/inscope_units.jsonl is THE single source of truth for 'is this unit in scope'. Any downstream disposition that marks a unit OOS/vendored/trusted/interface (unhunted-surface-adjudicate, exploit-class-coverage, trusted-infra-compromise, per-finding-oos, and their Go/Rust/Cosmos siblings) MUST consult it via tools/scope_authority.py - an in-scope FIRST-PARTY unit can never be OOS, whatever library it imports/extends (the strata bug closed the in-scope governance ACL as 'vendored' on a bare 'OpenZeppelin' substring). steps.business_flow_note: BUSINESS-FLOW DECOMPOSITION (2026-07-01, combination-bug axis, all-languages): beyond per-function and per-invariant decomposition, `business_flow_decompose.py` groups the in-scope units into cross-module flow slices of five types {operation | asset-lifecycle | state-machine | long-transaction | invariant} by lifecycle VERB + shared state (verbs are language-neutral: a Go MsgWithdraw, Rust withdraw, Solidity withdraw() all cluster). It emits .auditooor/business_flows.jsonl and, at the audit-complete tail, flags any DRIVABLE flow no hunt/harness touched (warn-undriven-flows). steps.label: Fork-modified scope prune (AUTOMATIC at Step-1 emit; author SCOPE.md "## Fork Bases")
steps.what_must_be_done: ONLY when src/ contains a fork of an upstream repo (bor<-go-ethereum, cosmos-sdk<-cosmos/cosmos-sdk, cometbft<-cometbft/cometbft, etc.): unmodified upstream is OOS. As of the 2026-06-22 keystone this is AUTOMATIC - you do NOT run a separate prune. The ONLY manual input is a "## Fork Bases" section in SCOPE.md, one row per fork: `local_name = owner/repo@ref` (e.g. `bor = ethereum/go-ethereum@v1.16.8`). Find the ref with `git -C src/<fork> log --grep "Merge tag 'v"`. Then `make audit` / `make audit-depth` run tools/resolve-fork-bases.py (-> .auditooor/fork_bases.json) and the inscope-manifest emit drops unmodified-upstream files automatically (multi-language, whitespace-normalized) BEFORE any candidate/engine/cert stage. Completeness-safe: a missing Fork Bases row -> the whole fork is KEPT + a loud WARN (never a silent under-scope). The standalone tools/fork-modified-files-scope.py / tools/resolve-fork-bases.py CLIs remain for manual/one-off use.

artifact_checks.note: manifest still populated after the prune

steps.label: make dataflow-slice (native inter-procedural def-use slice -> .auditooor/dataflow_paths.jsonl)
steps.what_must_be_done: Run `make dataflow-slice WS={workspace}` as the required cross-language def-use substrate. The router must materialize a compiler or IR-backed semantic receipt and .auditooor/dataflow_paths.jsonl for every supported language in the authoritative in-scope inventory: Slither for Solidity, Go SSA for Go, and rustc MIR for Rust. JavaScript, TypeScript, and Oscript enumeration are not semantic substitutes; an applicable language without an approved backend is a terminal unsupported-applicable result that must be repaired before Step 2. The substrate records source-to-impact-sink paths, inter-procedural hops, guard domination, storage-mediated flows, and language-specific semantic edges consumed by later reasoners. A missing language arm, timeout, compile failure, malformed output, or empty applicable substrate is terminal.

artifact_checks.note: The semantic dataflow artifact remains required for every non-Oscript applicable language. Oscript must instead emit its selected ast-backed/syntactic parser receipt through tools/oscript-ast-substrate.py. That receipt grants no semantic-engine, depth, or fuzz credit; it cannot satisfy any other language's dataflow requirement. Missing or empty selected artifacts are FIRE-CHECK failures.

steps.label: Deep semantic engine substrates (compiler/IR-backed only, before reasoners)

Regex, AST-only, graph-only, and enumerator-only evidence cannot credit an applicable language. This step must not author harnesses, run fuzz campaigns, consume reasoner obligations, or emit a depth certificate.

steps.label: LOGIC #2 asymmetric ROUNDING-DIRECTION DIRECTIONAL DIFFERENCE (ERC-4626 preview/convert rounding-direction / share-price truncation): {value conversions whose rounding mode(V) VIOLATES the protocol-favoring owed-direction D(V)} + {mirror pairs whose two legs round the SAME direction}
steps.what_must_be_done: LOAD-BEARING PRE-HUNT LOGIC REASONER (runs BEFORE step-3, AFTER the dataflow slice step-1c). Run `python3 tools/directional-rounding-asymmetry.py --workspace <ws>`. READS: the intra-repo call-graph + per-fn body substrate (self-built over source; corroborated by dataflow_paths.jsonl value-flow sinks when present). For each entrypoint-reachable value-conversion node it tags the rounding MODE (down/floor via integer div or mulDiv-floor, up/ceil via mulDivRoundingUp / +denominator-1 idiom) and the owed-DIRECTION relative to the protocol (owes-out = a quantity the protocol PAYS the user, takes-in = one it COLLECTS) from the fn's protocol role, then computes the directional difference: a survivor is any conversion whose mode VIOLATES the favoring direction (owes-out rounds up, or takes-in rounds down) OR a mirror pair (deposit/withdraw, mint/redeem, convertToShares/convertToAssets) whose two legs round the SAME direction (round-trip protection broken). When direction or mode cannot be statically confirmed the node is emitted advisory_only=needs_source (under --emit-advisory), never silently dropped. FEEDS: emits directional_rounding_asymmetry_obligations.jsonl (one row per survivor - a value leg that rounds AGAINST the protocol so a sub-wei residual compounds over a batch / repeated call) which tools/exploit-queue.py ingests into the exploit_queue (directional_rounding_asymmetry) AND tools/per-fn-mimo-batch-gen.py folds into the per-fn OPEN-OBLIGATIONS block so the step-3 hunt is STEERED by the reasoned obligation. how_to_verify_done: the obligation jsonl exists (reasoner RAN) AND every emitted obligation reaches a TERMINAL verdict consumed by exploit_queue (enforced fail-closed at step-5 by the logic-obligation-resolution signal, tools/logic-obligation-resolution-check.py).
